The speed/category of Milton is so concerning to forecasters because the more powerful the storm over sea, the larger the surge. It will flood to levels never seen before and have power behind it, leading to inundations over historic markers. If you can get to a higher elevation, please do. The 12 ft elevation of your house, vs a 10-15 inundation ? Your house will not win, you will be left stranded. The water level alone will flood the house, but the power (basically the speed of the water) will be high. The water does not rise slowly and still, it will push at pillars, foundations, all structural elements. Even to find somewhere 5-10 miles inland, at 20 elevation or higher, would be better.

with storm surge the ocean will just be that much higher. So if Milton goes slightly north and we see that 15 ft surge, you will be waist deep in water and unable to open your door to get out. It doesn’t really matter if you’re one or two miles in if you’re that low.
evacuate

if predicted surge is 12-15 ft, it could be more or less than that height. That is just the water rise.... you STILL have crashing waves on TOP of that. It's an absolutely nightmare when you are in the path of the eye AND have surge. You had best get out.

the eye and south of it bring the most surge. a wobble north and we are closer to a direct hit, more surge. a wobble south means we will get more of the north east side of the storm, which will pull the water out, less surge. we want to be as far north of the eye as possible! hurricanes rotate counterclockwise so you can see the movement of water in and out if you look at how it spins
